refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target throughout the operational period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (link availability) and ties it explicitly to the customer-defined target and operational timeframe, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime and downtime over the evaluation period using telemetry data to verify availability meets or exceeds the target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor network link status continuously to detect availability issues.
FR2: Manage network link recovery and failover to maintain availability.
FR3: Report link availability status to relevant system components and stakeholders.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement redundant network communication paths with automatic failover.
DP2: Use real-time link health monitoring and predictive analytics.
DP3: Integrate link status reporting and alerting mechanisms.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement redundant network communication paths with automatic failover.

potential_sources: Link monitoring tightly coupled with failover mechanisms
Reporting depends on link status data
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically supports the sub-FRs with manageable coupling; redundancy and failover are core to availability, while monitoring and reporting are supportive but modular.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover.
Risk of increased system complexity impacting maintainability.
Risk that failover mechanisms may introduce latency affecting operator experience.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding DP2 and DP3 as supporting DPs in subsequent decomposition levels to address monitoring and reporting explicitly.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical and embedded systems responsible for network communication hardware and failover logic.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for physical and logical network communication paths supporting redundancy and failover.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to send link status and availability data to monitoring and reporting modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: High-level system scope; contains overall solution but less direct control over physical link redundancy.
Primary containment of network hardware and failover logic; best fit for implementing DP1.
Receives link status but does not control physical link redundancy.
Minimal involvement; may receive alerts but not responsible for link availability.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Implementing link redundancy and failover within the Vehicle module centralizes control of physical network components, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential complexity in Vehicle module may increase integration effort.
Dependency on external network infrastructure may limit control.
Coordination with Teleoperation module needed for status reporting.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate integration interfaces with Teleoperation for status reporting and consider modularizing monitoring functions in Teleoperation.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (redundant network paths with failover) directly addresses the refined FR sub-functions and meets availability requirements.

diffusion_risks: Failover logic complexity within Vehicle may increase module diffusion if not well encapsulated.
Status reporting requires clear interface definitions to Teleoperation.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module is the best containment candidate for physical network redundancy; interfaces to Teleoperation and NGHE Solution are well defined.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and network link monitoring is feasible within assigned IM boundaries.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of physical network redundancy to Vehicle and status reporting interfaces to Teleoperation.

recommended_revisions: Refine interface definitions between Vehicle and Teleoperation for link status reporting.
Plan for modular monitoring and alerting functions in Teleoperation in next decomposition level.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition and assignments are sound, further refinement of monitoring and reporting functions as separate DPs and IMs is needed to fully address all sub-FRs and reduce diffusion risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor network link status continuously to detect availability issues.
FR2: Manage network link recovery and failover to maintain availability.
FR3: Report link availability status to relevant system components and stakeholders.

revise.why: To explicitly include monitoring and reporting DPs supporting the primary failover DP, improving modularity and clarity.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover.
Telemetry data is reliable for verification.
Vehicle module can encapsulate failover logic effectively.
open_risks: Integration complexity between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
Potential latency introduced by failover mechanisms.
Ensuring customer-defined availability targets are met under all conditions.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data demonstrating link availability over evaluation period.
Test results of failover mechanisms under simulated network failures.
Interface validation between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
